description: Extracts the five primary SEC consolidated financial statements (Balance Sheet, Statements of Operations/Income, Comprehensive Income, Changes in Equity, Cash Flows) while preserving visual hierarchy: section headers, indentation level, parent line item, subtotals, and per-period values. Handles multi-page tables: when a statement spans multiple pages (continuation with or without repeated headers, or multiple sub-tables for different periods), merge all parts into a SINGLE complete table with all rows. Explicitly excludes MD&A reconciliations, FFO/NOI tables, rent rolls, and other non-primary schedules.

financialTables.description: Primary consolidated financial statement tables only. INCLUDE: Consolidated Balance Sheet(s), Consolidated Statements of Operations, Consolidated Statements of Income, Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income, Consolidated Statements of (Changes in) Stockholders' Equity, Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows. EXCLUDE: MD&A variance/comparison tables (e.g. 'Comparison of the Three Months Ended...'), FFO/NOI reconciliations, property-level operating metrics (rent rolls, lease expirations, customer concentration, capital expenditure rollforwards), debt schedules, segment breakdowns, notes-to-the-financial-statements detail tables, and any narrative/textual tables. MULTI-PAGE TABLE HANDLING: Financial statements often span multiple pages. You MUST merge all continuation parts into a SINGLE complete table entry. Specifically: (A) If a table is cut mid-flow and continues on the next page WITHOUT repeated headers, concatenate all rows into one table. (B) If a table continues on the next page WITH repeated column headers, ignore the repeated headers and concatenate the data rows into one table. (C) If a section contains multiple sub-tables with different column headers for different periods (e.g. Equity roll-forward with 'Nine months ended September 30, 2024' and 'Nine months ended September 30, 2023'), emit ONE array entry per sub-table — each with its own period caption, headers, and rows. NEVER emit incomplete or truncated tables. ALWAYS include ALL rows from the first row to the final total/balance row. BALANCE SHEET CLASSIFICATION: 'Stockholders' equity' or 'Equity' sections that appear WITHIN a Balance Sheet are part of the Balance Sheet — they are NOT separate Equity statements. Only standalone 'Statements of Changes in Stockholders' Equity' (roll-forward tables showing beginning balance, transactions, ending balance) are Equity statements.

tableTitle.description: The exact title or caption printed above the table, verbatim. For Equity roll-forward sub-tables, include both the statement title and the period caption (e.g. 'Consolidated Statements of Equity — Nine months ended September 30, 2024').

companyName.description: Registrant name as printed above the table, verbatim (e.g. 'Safehold Inc.').

statementType.description: Category of the financial statement. Choose exactly one based on the table title: CashFlow (Statements of Cash Flows), BalanceSheet (Balance Sheet / Statement of Financial Position — note: the 'Stockholders Equity' section that appears at the bottom of a Balance Sheet is PART of the Balance Sheet, NOT a separate Equity statement), IncomeStatement (Statements of Operations or Statements of Income - the primary income statement only, NEVER MD&A variance tables or FFO/NOI reconciliations), ComprehensiveIncome (Statements of Comprehensive Income), Equity (ONLY standalone Statements of Changes in Stockholders' Equity — roll-forward tables showing opening balance, period transactions like net income/dividends/stock comp, and closing balance), Other (anything that is not one of the five primary statements).

unit.description: Reporting unit as printed, verbatim (e.g. 'thousands', 'millions', 'thousands, except per share data').

periodHeaders.description: LEAF (bottom-most) column headers in STRICT physical left-to-right order as they appear on the page. One entry per value column. Do NOT include the line-item label column. CRITICAL: When a table has multi-level (grouped) headers, the leaf columns under the LEFTMOST group come FIRST, then the next group's leaves, and so on — matching the physical page layout. Example: if the page shows 'Common Stock' spanning two sub-columns ('Number of Shares', 'Amount') on the LEFT, followed by 'Additional Paid-in Capital', 'Deferred Compensation Plan', 'Retained Earnings', 'Total' to the RIGHT, then periodHeaders MUST be ['Number of Shares','Amount','Additional Paid-in Capital','Deferred Compensation Plan','Retained Earnings','Total'] — the grouped sub-columns appear first because they are physically leftmost. NEVER reorder columns or push grouped sub-columns to the end. Values in each row MUST follow this same physical left-to-right column order.

periodGroupHeaders.description: SPANNING (top-level) header for each corresponding leaf column in periodHeaders[], in the SAME physical left-to-right order. One entry per leaf column — repeat the group name for every leaf it spans, use empty string '' for ungrouped columns. Length MUST ALWAYS equal periodHeaders[] length. Example: Equity table where 'Common Stock' spans 'Number of Shares' and 'Amount' (the two leftmost columns), followed by 5 ungrouped columns → ['Common Stock','Common Stock','','','','','']. Income Statement where 'Three Months Ended Sep 30,' spans 2024/2023 and 'Nine Months Ended Sep 30,' spans 2024/2023 → ['Three Months Ended Sep 30,','Three Months Ended Sep 30,','Nine Months Ended Sep 30,','Nine Months Ended Sep 30,']. ALWAYS populate this array when the table has a two-level header structure. Leave empty ONLY when there is truly a single header row (e.g. a Balance Sheet with just date columns).

rows.description: ALL data rows in EXACT top-to-bottom order as they appear on the printed page, merging across pages if the table spans multiple pages. CRITICAL ORDERING RULE: Read rows by scanning the page from top to bottom. A section header and ALL its indented children MUST appear together — children immediately follow their parent header, BEFORE the next sibling at the same or higher level. Example for a Balance Sheet: 'ASSETS' (L0 header), then 'Net investment in sales-type leases' (L1), then 'Ground Lease receivables' (L1), then 'Real estate' (L1 sub-header), then 'Real estate, at cost' (L2 child of Real estate), then 'Less: accumulated depreciation' (L2), then 'Real estate, net' (L2 subtotal), then 'Total real estate...' (L1 subtotal), then 'Loans receivable' (L1 next sibling), etc. NEVER group or sort rows by level — preserve the exact visual reading order. The table MUST be complete — from the first line item to the final total/balance row. For Cash Flow statements, this means ALL three sections (operating, investing, financing) PLUS the beginning/ending cash reconciliation rows. For Balance Sheets, this means ALL assets, ALL liabilities, AND the stockholders' equity section through 'Total liabilities and stockholders' equity'. For multi-page tables, concatenate rows from all continuation pages. NEVER truncate or omit rows. EVERY row MUST have a non-empty lineItem.

lineItem.description: The row label from the first column, verbatim. REQUIRED for every row — never leave blank. Keep original casing and punctuation including trailing ':'. For Equity roll-forward tables, typical labels include 'Balance as of December 31, 2023', 'Net income', 'Issuance of common stock, net of issuance costs of $X', 'Stock-based compensation', 'Common stock dividends ($X.XX per share)', etc. Repeating labels across quarters (e.g. 'Net income' appearing in Q1, Q2, Q3) is expected and correct.

level.description: Visual indentation level of this row. Classify as: '0' = top-level section header (e.g. 'Assets', 'REVENUES', 'Cash flows from operating activities:') or top-level total/balance row (e.g. 'Total assets', 'Balance as of December 31, 2023'). '1' = direct child of a level-0 section (e.g. 'Cash and cash equivalents', 'Net income', individual line items under a section header). '2' = child of a level-1 sub-section (e.g. 'Depreciation' under 'Adjustments to reconcile...'). '3' = deeper nesting. Subtotals share the level of the section they summarize. EVERY row MUST be classified — never leave empty.

parentLineItem.description: lineItem of the nearest enclosing section or sub-section header. Empty string for level-0 rows.

isSectionHeader.description: True for section and sub-section header rows (e.g. 'Cash flows from operating activities:', 'Adjustments to reconcile net income to ...'). For Equity roll-forward tables, there are typically no section headers — set false for all rows. ALWAYS set this field for every row — do NOT leave it empty.

isSubtotal.description: True if this row is a subtotal or total of the preceding indented block (e.g. 'Total assets', 'Net cash provided by operating activities', 'Balance, December 31, 2024'). FOR EQUITY ROLL-FORWARD TABLES: ALL 'Balance as of ...' rows are subtotals (isSubtotal=true). ALWAYS set this field for every row — do NOT leave it empty.

values.description: Cell values left-to-right, verbatim as printed (keep '$', commas, parentheses for negatives, '—' for blanks). One entry per column in periodHeaders[]; length MUST equal periodHeaders[] length. For Equity roll-forward tables, each row's values correspond to equity component columns (shares, amounts, etc.).
